response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web engineer with ~15 years of experience, but the profile is a poor match for a Principal Performance Architect role centered on SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance, and hardware-software co-design. There is no evidence of GPU/SIMD/MIMD architecture work, deep-learning workload optimization, or silicon-level performance analysis; the closest adjacent experience is low-latency DLang services and brief C/C++ work at National Instruments, which falls well short of the principal-level hardware architecture depth required.
strengths: Long, consistent engineering career (15+ years) satisfying the BS + 8 years experience path
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching) handling millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby
Strong software craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source authorship, and technical teaching/workshops
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware performance-modeling experience
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/accelerator workload analysis background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not the principal-level hardware-software co-design this role demands
